meta.subject: Henslee paternal line — full trace from Hunter Spence's grandmother Alice Marie Henslee Spence (1936) to earliest documented ancestor
meta.scope: Direct Henslee paternal line only. Collateral branches documented only where needed to establish the chain.
meta.privacy_note: No living persons named. All individuals born before 1936 or confirmed deceased.

confidence_tiers.CONFIRMED: Supported by a cited primary source (will, deed, census, burial record, contemporary biography)
confidence_tiers.PROBABLE: Strong inference from 2+ corroborating secondary sources, or primary source with minor ambiguity
confidence_tiers.POSSIBLE: Plausible from one secondary source or logical census inference
confidence_tiers.UNVERIFIED: Asserted in family tradition or derivative source only; not corroborated

summary.earliest_confirmed_ancestor: Macksfield Henslee (Hensley), born ~1725 in Virginia Colony (prob. Hanover Co.), died before Aug 1801 in Caswell Co., North Carolina
summary.earliest_ancestor_primary_source: Caswell Co., NC Will Book D, p. 70 — will witnessed 31 Dec 1794, proved Oct Court 1801; names sons John and David explicitly
summary.immigrant_status: Macksfield is NOT himself the immigrant — he was born in Virginia Colony (appeared on Hanover Co. VA tax list 1763). The English immigrant ancestor is at least one generation earlier, identity UNVERIFIED.
summary.country_of_origin: England — surname traces to Devon/Norfolk England. First English documentary record: John de Henselay, Yorkshire Subsidy Rolls 1297. HouseOfNames cites Devon as surname origin locale.
summary.american_immigrant_earliest_documented: Jone Hensley, Virginia 1702 (cited HouseOfNames); not proven connected to this Macksfield line.
notable_figures: Lee W. Henslee (1873-?): Sheriff of Burleson County TX, repeatedly elected 1902-1908 without opposition — documented in 1910 Texas historical publication
Macksfield Henslee: DAR/SAR qualifying ancestor — paid for Revolutionary War service (Haun NC Rev Army Accts Vol XI p.1325, certificate #431)
Enoch Henslee (Gen 4, 1832-aft.1880): Confederate soldier (CSA Veteran marker, Little Saline Cemetery, Menard Co TX, per Find a Grave #20814832)
summary.dna_note: Y-chromosome haplogroup I-M253 confirmed for Franco Hensley (Maxfield line) via FamilyTreeDNA kit #213697 — 37-marker test. This is the direct paternal haplogroup of the Henslee line.

direct_line.role_in_chain: Earliest confirmed Henslee ancestor in America
direct_line.name: Macksfield Henslee
direct_line.birth_notes: 1786 NC State census gives age 'over 60', implying born before 1726. First record is 31 Dec 1757 Fredericksville Parish, Louisa Co VA deed witness. Listed on 1763 Hanover Co VA tax list. Carolshouse.com: 'first appears in 1763 as owner of 150 acres in what is now Hanover County, VA.'
direct_line.death_source: Caswell Co NC Will Book D, p. 82 — inventory of estate of Macksfield Henslee, 10 Aug 1801 by executor David Henslee
direct_line.spouse_notes: Married ~1752 probably in Virginia. 'Sneed' maiden name is family tradition (Cindy Stamps research); not proven from primary source. Martha died after 31 Dec 1794 (named in will as recipient) and before will probate Oct 1801.
direct_line.residence_trail: Hanover Co VA (1757-1763) → Caswell Co NC (by 1777, per tax list) → remained Caswell Co until death
primary_sources: Caswell Co NC Will Book D, p. 70 — will dated 31 Dec 1794, proved Oct Court 1801
Caswell Co NC Will Book D, p. 82 — estate inventory 10 Aug 1801
Caswell Co NC Deed Book J, p. 68 — 'Macksfield Henslee of Caswell County to David Henslee of same', 25 Apr 1795 (confirms David as son)
Caswell Co NC Deed Book J, p. 84 — 'Macksfield Henslee to John Henslee of same', 23 Apr 1795 (confirms John as son)
Haun NC Rev Army Accts Vol XI p. 1325, certificate #431 — paid for Revolutionary War service (DAR/SAR qualifying)
direct_line.confidence_notes: Primary sources confirm identity, residence, death, and children. Birth year is PROBABLE from census age evidence.

direct_line.role_in_chain: Son of Macksfield; proven in father's will
direct_line.name: David Henslee
direct_line.birth_notes: Carolshouse.com and martygrant.com both place birth ~1755-1765 in Virginia. WikiTree Henslee-88 shows 'David Henslee 1765-1825 Hanover, Hanover, Colony of Virginia'.
direct_line.death_place: Franklin County, Georgia (probable) or Cherokee Co., Alabama
direct_line.death_notes: Last appears on Franklin Co GA tax lists through ~1821; not found thereafter. Martygrant.com: 'died aft 1820 in Franklin Co, GA'.
direct_line.spouse_notes: Maiden name 'Payne' is family tradition per Cindy Stamps; married before ~1786 in Caswell Co NC. Not confirmed from primary source.
direct_line.residence_trail: Caswell Co NC (to ~1818) → Franklin Co GA (~1817-1821) → Cherokee Co AL (possible, per some sources)
primary_sources: Caswell Co NC Will Book D, p. 70 — named as son of Macksfield in 1794 will
Caswell Co NC Deed Book J, p. 68 — David Henslee receives 65 acres from father Macksfield, 25 Apr 1795
Caswell Co NC Will Book D, p. 82 — David Henslee acts as executor of Macksfield's estate, 10 Aug 1801
direct_line.confidence_notes: Named explicitly in father's will and deed records as son. Birth place/year are PROBABLE.

direct_line.role_in_chain: Son of David (probable); grandfather of Texas Henslee line
direct_line.name: Enoch Henslee
direct_line.birth_notes: 1850 census gives age 64, implying birth ~1785/86. Born in NC per 1850 census. 'Undoubtedly born in Caswell County' per martygrant.com. Parentage from David is PROBABLE not confirmed — martygrant.com: 'It seems very likely that Enoch was a son of David Hensley... but I don't have solid proof.'
direct_line.death_notes: Last court record is 21 Oct 1853, Saline Co AR. Not found in 1860 census. Martygrant.com.
direct_line.spouse_source: 1850 Census Mortality Schedule (primary); Caswell Co NC marriage bond 27 Oct 1806 (primary)
direct_line.marriage_source: Caswell Co NC marriage bond, 27 Oct 1806 (primary source)
direct_line.residence_trail: Caswell/Rockingham Co NC (to ~1818) → Franklin Co GA (~1818-1823) → Hall Co GA (~1823-1840s) → Carroll Co GA (~1840s) → Saline Co AR (~1844-1853+)
primary_sources: Caswell Co NC marriage bond, 27 Oct 1806 — Enoch Henslee to Amy Hasten
1810 Census Rockingham Co NC — 'Enoch Hensley' household, consistent with wife + young son age 0-10
1830 Census Hall Co GA — 'Enoch Hensley' household
1840 Census Carroll Co GA — 'Enoch Hensley' household
1850 Census Saline Co AR, p. 124, house/family 591/599 — Enoch Hensley 64 M W Farmer (Widowed) NC
1850 Census Mortality Schedule — Amy Hensley (Horton) age 63, born VA, died Dropsy, Saline Co AR Jan 1850
direct_line.confidence_notes: Marriage bond and census records confirm the individual. Parentage from David is PROBABLE not CONFIRMED — no primary source names David as Enoch's father. Strong circumstantial evidence: same counties, same naming patterns (son named 'David', son named 'Mackfield').

direct_line.role_in_chain: Son of Enoch; first Henslee to arrive in Texas
direct_line.name: Maxfield G. Henslee
direct_line.birth_notes: Born in NC per census records. 1810 Rockingham Co NC census shows Enoch Henslee with one apparent son 0-10. Born ~1809/10 per age in later census records. Martygrant.com notes NC birthplace.
direct_line.death_notes: Find a Grave memorial #20814832: 'Birth 1810, Death Jul 1887', buried Little Saline Cemetery, Menard County TX. WikiTree Henslee-88: 'Died 1887 at about age 77 in London, Texas.'
direct_line.residence_trail: Carroll Co GA (~1830) → Saline Co AR (~1844-1851) → Johnson Co TX (~1851) → Hood/Collin Co TX → Menard/Kimble Co TX (died)
direct_line.texas_arrival_notes: Hood County biography of son Maxfield F. Henslee states 'His father, Maxfield Henslee, a native of Georgia, came to Texas in 1851, and after a short stay in Hunt County bought land in Collin County, and from there moved to Hood County.'
direct_line.csa_service: Confederate States of America veteran — CSA marker at Little Saline Cemetery, Menard Co TX
children_with_phoebe_campbell: Maxfield F. Henslee (1849-1933) — Hood County TX
children_with_mary_blackwell: Enoch Henslee (1832-1916) — Burleson Co TX [KEY ANCESTOR for Lee Stuart Henslee line]
primary_sources: Find a Grave memorial #20814832 — Maxfield G. Henslee, burial Little Saline Cemetery Menard Co TX, birth 1810 death Jul 1887
1880 US Census Burleson Co TX — confirms Enoch Henslee son in TX
Hood County TX biography (published 1910): 'His father, Maxfield Henslee, a native of Georgia, came to Texas in 1851'
direct_line.confidence_notes: Find a Grave primary for death. 1910 biographical publication primary for Texas arrival. Parentage from Enoch is PROBABLE (census-based).

direct_line.role_in_chain: Son of Maxfield (by first wife Mary Blackwell); probable grandfather or great-uncle of Lee Stuart Henslee (1908)
direct_line.name: Enoch Henslee
direct_line.birth_notes: Listed on 1880 Burleson Co TX census, age ~48, born GA. Martygrant.com: 'born 1832/33 in Carroll Co, GA'.
direct_line.death_notes: Last found on 1880 census Burleson Co TX. Find a Grave #20814832 lists him as child of Maxfield but with dates 1832-1916. He married Sarah J (maiden name unknown), born 1832/33 in GA.
primary_sources: 1880 US Census Burleson Co TX — Enoch Henslee household
Find a Grave #20814832 — listed as child of Maxfield G. Henslee
direct_line.confidence_notes: Identity confirmed by census and Find a Grave. Role as ancestor of Lee Stuart Henslee (1908) is POSSIBLE — direct linkage not proven by primary source.

direct_line.role_in_chain: Probable uncle of Lee Stuart Henslee's grandfather; documented Burleson County Henslee
direct_line.name: Lee W. Henslee

parents.mother: Sarah J. (maiden name unknown, born Georgia)
parents.source: 1910 Texas historical publication — 'His parents were Enoch and Sarah (Hudson) Henslee, both natives of Georgia'

direct_line.note_on_mother_surname: The 1910 publication gives mother's maiden name as 'Hudson'; but martygrant.com and other sources give the wife as 'Sarah J.' with unknown maiden name. 'Hudson' may be accurate from a biographical interview.
direct_line.occupation: Sheriff of Burleson County, Texas — appointed June 1902, elected 1902, 1904, 1906, 1908 (unopposed)
direct_line.notable: Biographical entry in Historical Review of South-East Texas and the Founders, Leaders and Representative Men, Vol. 2 (Lewis Publishing Co., Chicago, 1910). Repeated election as sheriff is documented public service record.
primary_sources: Historical Review of South-East Texas Vol. 2, Lewis Publishing Co. Chicago 1910 — full biographical entry
http://genealogytrails.com/tex/state/bio_se_tex_v2_7.html (transcription of 1910 biography)
direct_line.confidence_notes: 1910 published biography is a near-primary source naming parents. Linkage of this Lee W. Henslee (b.1873) to Lee Stuart Henslee (b.1908) is NOT proven — they share first name and Burleson/Jefferson County TX geography but are likely of the same Enoch (1832) branch.

direct_line.name: Lee Stuart Henslee
direct_line.birth_place_notes: Alice Marie Henslee born 1936 in Rusk TX (Cherokee County) — Lee Stuart likely in East Texas. Birth date from PeopleLegacy burial record.
direct_line.burial_place: Forest Lawn Memorial Park and Funeral Home, Beaumont, Jefferson County, Texas
primary_sources: https://peoplelegacy.com/lee_s_henslee-571W0P — burial record with birth and death dates
direct_line.parent_connection_note: Parents of Lee Stuart Henslee NOT YET identified from primary source. Probable connection to Enoch (Gen 5) branch of Burleson/East Texas Henslee cluster. Best free research path: Texas Death Certificate for Lee Stuart Henslee (names parents); 1920/1930 US Census for Lee Henslee household in East TX.

direct_line.name: Frances Virginia Henslee (née Rau)
direct_line.burial_place: Forest Lawn Memorial Park and Funeral Home, Beaumont, Jefferson County, Texas
primary_sources: Find a Grave memorial #39700994 — Frances Henslee burial record
https://peoplelegacy.com/frances_henslee-3j7a3f
https://www.ancientfaces.com/directory/name/frances-henslee
direct_line.confidence_notes: Multiple burial record sources confirm dates and burial. Maiden name 'Rau' from existing research file 42.

direct_line.name: Alice Marie Henslee Spence
direct_line.burial_place: Forest Lawn Memorial Park and Funeral Home, Beaumont, Jefferson County, Texas
direct_line.parents_in_source: Lee S. Henslee + Frances Henslee (confirmed co-buried at Forest Lawn — same section)
primary_sources: https://peoplelegacy.com/alice_marie_henslee_spence-3F0H76

surname_origin.etymology: Habitational surname from Hensley in East Worlington, Devon, England. Old English: 'Hēahmundes lēah' (Hēahmund's woodland clearing) per Ancestry/Dictionary of American Family Names 2022. Alternate etymology: Old English 'Hengest' (stallion) + 'leah' (clearing) — 'stallion meadow' — per multiple sources.
surname_origin.earliest_English_record: John de Henselay, Yorkshire Subsidy Rolls, 1297 (cited HouseOfNames)
surname_origin.origin_county_primary: Devon, England (Hensley village in East Worlington parish)
surname_origin.origin_county_secondary: Norfolk, England (Hensley hamlet) — separate possible origin locale
surname_origin.variant_note: The 'Henslee' spelling is specific to and predominantly found in the Macksfield Henslee line of Caswell Co NC and their Texas descendants. Other American branches predominantly used 'Hensley'.

american_immigrants_documented.name: Jone Hensley
american_immigrants_documented.connection_to_direct_line: UNVERIFIED — not proven related to Macksfield Henslee

american_immigrants_documented.name: Robert Hensley
american_immigrants_documented.source: coadb.com Hensley coat of arms page

american_immigrants_documented.name: William Hinsley (Hensley)
american_immigrants_documented.arrival: Virginia, 1675 — Thomas Wood land patent, Rappahannock Co VA, for importing William Hinsley
american_immigrants_documented.connection_to_direct_line: POSSIBLE — martygrant.com researcher believes this may be ancestor of the Macksfield line, but no proof

macksfield_parentage.claim: WikiTree (citing William Hensley ~1691-1781 profile) places Macksfield's father as William Hensley (c.1691-c.1781) of Fluvanna County, Colony of Virginia, son of James Hensley II (1642-1705) of Northampton VA, whose father was James Henry Hensley (1605-1665) of Tiverton, Devon, England
macksfield_parentage.caveat: This chain (William → Macksfield) is asserted on WikiTree Hensley-196 and Henslee-88 but the researcher at martygrant.com explicitly states 'I have no clue who Mackfield's parents might be.' The WikiTree chain for pre-Macksfield generations is UNVERIFIED for this direct line.

surname_origin.confidence: CONFIRMED (Devon/Yorkshire English origin); POSSIBLE (specific pre-Macksfield chain)

notable_figures.name: Macksfield Henslee (~1725-1801)
notable_figures.notable_for: DAR/SAR qualifying ancestor — paid for Revolutionary War service per Haun NC Rev Army Accts Vol XI p. 1325, certificate #431. Land grant recipient Caswell Co NC 1778. Planted tobacco plantation on Stoney Creek.
notable_figures.source: carolshouse.com citing Haun NC Revolutionary Army Accounts

notable_figures.name: Maxfield G. Henslee (~1810-1887)
notable_figures.notable_for: Confederate States Army veteran. Brought the Henslee family to Texas ~1851. Buried Little Saline Cemetery, Menard Co TX with CSA marker.
notable_figures.source: Find a Grave memorial #20814832; Hood County TX biography (1910)

notable_figures.name: Lee W. Henslee (1873-?)
notable_figures.notable_for: Sheriff of Burleson County, Texas. Appointed June 1902; elected 1902, 1904, 1906; re-elected 1908 without opposition. Son of Enoch Henslee (Gen 5, Burleson Co TX) and Sarah Hudson. Married Lucile Chambers 1900.
notable_figures.source: Historical Review of South-East Texas Vol. 2, Lewis Publishing Co. Chicago 1910

immigrant_to_america.status: NOT CONFIRMED for direct Henslee line
immigrant_to_america.explanation: Macksfield Henslee (~1725) appears to be American-born (born Virginia Colony). His parents are UNVERIFIED. The immigrant ancestor to the Macksfield Henslee line is at least one — possibly two — generations earlier. Multiple Hensley/Henslee immigrants arrived in Virginia colony between 1642 and 1702; none are proven direct ancestors of Macksfield.
best_candidates: William Hinsley — imported to Rappahannock Co VA 1675 (land patent evidence)
William Hensley (~1583-1654) — Norfolk England origin, settled Virginia Colony per coadb.com (distant/speculative)
immigrant_to_america.english_county_of_origin: Devon (primary — Hensley village, East Worlington) or Norfolk (secondary — Hensley hamlet)
immigrant_to_america.confidence: POSSIBLE for Devon/Norfolk origin; UNVERIFIED for specific immigrant individual

dna_evidence.implication: If Hunter Spence's direct Henslee paternal line connects to the Macksfield branch, the expected Y-haplogroup is I-M253. Hunter's own Y-DNA comes via his Spence grandfather (English), not Henslee — Henslee is Hunter's paternal grandmother's maiden name.
